title: use localhost for host-gateway with --network=host
body:
### Feature request description

When using --network=host, podman automatically adds whatever IP address it can find to /etc/hosts as host.containers.local, but this has a few problems:
- if the host address change (e.g. DHCP lease renewal on a different address, roaming to a different network, whatever) then that host is no longer reachable
- if the host has no ip address configured (other than loopback) then no dns is added at all

The later can be reproduced with bwrap (sorry, this is a bit ugly as I normally use a wrapper for this...)
```
$ bwrap --unshare-net --unshare-user --unshare-ipc --unshare-pid --unshare-cgroup  --ro-bind /usr /usr --ro-bind /etc/containers /etc/containers --ro-bind /lib /lib --ro-bind /lib64 /lib64 --dev-bind /dev /dev --bind /sys/fs/cgroup /sys/fs/cgroup  --tmpfs $HOME --tmpfs /tmp --tmpfs /var/tmp --ro-bind ~/.config/containers ~/.config/containers --bind ~/.local/share/containers ~/.local/share/containers --proc /proc --ro-bind /etc/passwd /etc/passwd --ro-bind /etc/group /etc/group --ro-bind /run/ /run --tmpfs $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR --ro-bind $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/bus $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/bus  podman run --hosts-file none --network=host --rm -ti docker.io/alpine cat /etc/hosts
127.0.0.1	localhost
::1	localhost
```

### Suggest potential solution

In either case, with --network=host then localhost is reachable, so privileging 127.0.0.1 (or ::1 if ipv6 is enabled) seems more robust all around

### Have you considered any alternatives?

For the first problem (address change) I used to think the files could be kept up to date, but updating /etc/hosts dynamically when address changes doesn't seem better as there could just be no non-loopback addresses, so I can't think of any right now.
Ah, yes, I also tried setting host_containers_internal_ip but this setting is effective regardless of --network=host or not, so I don't think it's applicable here.
For now I suggested manually setting `--add-host='host.containers.internal:127.0.0.1'`

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
Memo from looking at the implem, I might just open a PR in a couple of weeks if this doesn't get any reply (no hurry - I understand new years season is a bit slow)

- The hosts generation is done in libnetwork, but for some reason the host IP is computed outside (through a libnetwork helper...) in libpod/container_internal_common.go `addHosts()` (or buildah equivalent run code)
- (FWIW at this point in `--network=host` mode I expected `c.config.NetMode.IsHost()` to be true, but NetMode is apparently an empty string so `IsHost()` returns false...)
- were I to implement this I'd consider patching `etchosts.HostContainersInternalOptions()`, but given the above I'm not quite sure what to look at to check if the network is in hosts mode -- we have `c.runtime.config` and `c.runtime.network` available, I'd assume this could be figured out from the later...
- (FWIW#2, libnetwork resolvconf/resolv.go computes `hostNS` by looping through `c.config.Spec.Linux.Namespaces` -- that looks quite more complex than what I would have been expecting, but we're using the namespaces to compute a different resolvConf path so hostNS is more of an afterthought and it doesn't look like this would be worth trying to unify...)
For determining whether we are host network, look at https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/container.go#L1428
I'll work on this issue. I'll submit a PR to use localhost (127.0.0.1) for host-gateway when --network=host is specified.
